gmi_articles.title: There is currently no evidence from randomised studies that influenza vaccine given to people with cystic fibrosis is of benefit to them.
Gut Bifidobacteria enrichment following oral Lactobacillus-supplementation is associated with clinical improvements in children with cystic fibrosis.
5-methyltehydrofolate (folic acid) and vitamin B12 ameliorate red blood cell abnormalities in pediatric patients with Cystic Fibrosis.
A high lipase pancreatic enzyme is superior to regular pancreatin in adult cystic fibrosis patients.
Acupuncture is effective for managing symptoms and illnesses associated with cystic fibrosis.
An 8-month treatment with omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA) has a positive effects, such as decreasing inflammation, in patients with cystic fibrosis.
An antioxidant formula increases important fat-soluble nutrients and antioxidants and reduces airway inflammation in patients with Cystic Fibrosis.
Annual influenza vaccination hampers the development of virus-specific CD8(+) T cell responses necessary to protect against influenza infection.
Antioxidant supplementation may have therapeutic value in the treatment of cystic fibrosis - Article 2.
Antioxidant supplementation may have therapeutic value in the treatment of cystic fibrosis - Article 2.
Beta-carotene supplementation ameliorates deficiencies and reduces oxidative stress in Cystic Fibrosis patients.
Bisphosphonates used for osteoporosis in patients with cystic fibrosis increases bone mineral density without increasing bone quality/strength, while causing bone pain and flu-like symptoms.
Bolus weekly vitamin D3 supplementation impacts gut and airway microbiota in adults with cystic fibrosis.
Candida albicans chronic colonisation in cystic fibrosis may be associated with inhaled antibiotics.
Choline-related supplements may improve status of children with Cystic Fibrosis
Coenzyme Q10 levels are low in children with cystic fibrosis, indicating that supplementation could be beneficial.
Corticosteroids contribute to muscle weakness in Cystic Fibrosis patients.
Cysteamine plus epigallocatechin gallate can be used for autophagy-dependent rescue of class II-mutated CFTR.

gmi_articles.title: Daily vitamin D supplementation was associated with reduced Aspergillus induced IL-13 responses.
Engaged spirituality supports pro-adherence determinants and behaviour in patients with cystic fibrosis.
Exposure to Proton pump inhibitors is associated with higher numbers of hospitalizations for pulmonary exacerbation in cystic fibrosis patients.
Flaxseed modulates inflammatory and oxidative stress biomarkers in cystic fibrosis.
Fractional exhalation nitric oxide changes in cystic fibrosis patients induced by compound honey syrup.
Glutathione improves clinical markers in cystic fibrosis patients.
High dose vitamin K is efficacious in correcting vitamin K deficiency in cystic fibrosis.
Improved glutathione status in young adult patients with cystic fibrosis supplemented with whey protein.
Inhaled glutathione improves symptoms in patients with cystic fibrosis.
Inhaled hypertonic saline accelerates mucus clearance and improves lung function in patients with cystic fibrosis.
Inhaled hypertonic saline significantly reduces the incidence of pulmonary exacerbations in patients with cystic fibrosis.
Inspiratory muscle training is associated with significant improvements in physical endurance and strength in patients with Cystic Fibrosis.
L-arginine improves exhaled nitric oxide and pulmonary function in patients with cystic fibrosis.
L-arginine supplementation improve nitric oxide formation in cystic fibrosis patients who exhibit decreased levels of exhaled nitric oxide.
L-ascorbate may offer potential as a therapeutic agent for the improvement of chronic rhinosinusitis in patients with cystic fibrosis.
Lactobacillus GG supplementation reduces pulmonary exacerberations and hospital admissions in patients with cystic fibrosis.
Listening to motivational music while walking elicits more positive affective response in patients with cystic fibrosis.
Low-dose ketone monoester administration in adults with cystic fibrosis.
Manuka honey versus saline sinus irrigation in the treatment of cystic fibrosis-associated chronic rhinosinusitis.
N-acetylcysteine increases the fluidity of airway mucus in patients with Cystic Fibrosis.
N-acetylcysteine reduces inflammation in patients with cystic fibrosis.
Omega-3 and gamma-linolenic fatty acids improve pulmonary status (lung function, respiratory exacerbations and antibiotic consumption), inflammatory and anthropometric parameters in adults with CF.
